x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces LPC's open landmark data (landmarks, designated buildings, historic districts, designation reports, permit history, violations) bound under one LP_NUMBER-keyed model, and adds file_permit_application — the net-new write workflow locked today inside the Salesforce Portico portal.

server.name: io.nyc.lpc
server.title: NYC Landmarks Preservation Commission (LPC)
server.description: Agent-native access to the NYC Landmarks Preservation Commission — individual/interior/scenic landmarks, the designated-building database (architect, style, materials, dates), historic districts, designation and archaeology reports, issued permit history, and landmark violations from NYC Open Data, plus the net-new ability to file a landmark permit application (Certificate of Appropriateness / No Effect) that today lives only in the Salesforce Portico portal.
server.instructions: Use find_landmarks / get_landmark (and get_landmark_buildings) to locate designations by borough, type, historic district, or LP number and drill into their buildings. find_buildings searches the architectural building database by architect, style, or lot. find_historic_districts covers area designations. find_designation_reports returns designation and archaeology reports. find_permits searches the OPEN, issued permit history (Certificates of Appropriateness / No Effect). find_violations covers landmark complaints and violations. file_permit_application (net-new) files a new LPC permit application; it requires an authenticated applicant and mutates state — confirm the property (address/BBL/LP number), permit type, and scope of work with the user before submitting, and note that Certificate of Appropriateness filings go to a public hearing.

tools.name: find_landmarks
tools.title: Find Landmarks
tools.description: Search NYC landmark designations (individual, interior, scenic) by borough, type, site status, historic district, BBL, and/or free text.

tools.name: get_landmark
tools.title: Get Landmark
tools.description: Fetch a landmark designation (status, dates, address, geography) by LP number.

tools.name: get_landmark_buildings
tools.title: Get Landmark Buildings
tools.description: List the designated buildings (with architect, style, materials, dates) within a landmark or historic district.

tools.name: find_buildings
tools.title: Find Buildings
tools.description: Search the LPC landmark & historic-district building database by borough, historic district, architect, style, or BBL.

tools.name: find_historic_districts
tools.title: Find Historic Districts
tools.description: List NYC historic districts by borough, site status, or free text.

tools.name: find_designation_reports
tools.title: Find Designation Reports
tools.description: List LPC designation reports and archaeology reports by type, borough, LP number, or free text.

tools.name: find_permits
tools.title: Find Permit History
tools.description: Search LPC's OPEN, issued permit history (Certificates of Appropriateness / No Effect and related permits) by borough, regulation type, work type, BBL, or period.

tools.name: find_violations
tools.title: Find Violations
tools.description: Search LPC landmark complaints and violations by borough, record type, status, or BBL.

tools.name: list_my_permit_applications
tools.title: List My Permit Applications
tools.description: List the authenticated applicant's own LPC permit applications and their status — today visible only inside the Salesforce Portico portal.

tools.name: file_permit_application
tools.title: File a Permit Application
tools.description: File an LPC landmark permit application (Certificate of Appropriateness, Certificate of No Effect, or Permit for Minor Work). NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — it lives only in the Salesforce Portico portal (portico.lpc.nyc.gov). Requires an authenticated applicant. Mutates state — confirm the property (address/BBL/LP number), permit type, and scope of work first; Certificate of Appropriateness filings go to a public hearing.
